title: Resume the top-level PR task at real post-deploy validation, then validate existing Codex connectivity completeness
objective: Phase3 deployment is complete and the server is now on the PR commit. Resume the top-level publication task at phase4_run_real_post_deploy_tests. First run real post-deploy validation for the frozen release scope on the updated server. Then, if phase4 passes, validate completeness of the already-onboarded Codex identity. Do not rebuild deployment, and do not modify Codex onboarding implementation.

mandatory_references.why: Proof that phase3 deployment succeeded and the server is on the PR commit.
Frozen release scope and deferred items.
Existing Codex identity to be validated after release verification.
Baseline reference for the existing Codex ingress/egress path.

required_work.description: Run real post-deploy validation for the frozen release scope on the updated server. Revalidate the release baseline rather than only checking API health.

Explicitly verify that the deployed frontend main view is readable in real usage. If Chinese UI text or workflow artifact UI is visibly garbled/encoded incorrectly, classify that as a release blocker immediately.
Only if phase4 passes, validate completeness of the already-onboarded Codex identity against the updated server. Use the existing identity only; do not modify its onboarding or bridge code.
Close the top-level task with a structured result for release validation and Codex connectivity completeness.

phase4_validation_matrix.description: API health remains OK on the deployed commit.
Manager-only bootstrap semantics still hold on the deployed server.
The frozen-scope simple formal workflow baseline can still complete on the deployed server.
Artifact main-view rendering remains correct and raw JSON does not dominate the UI.
Core UI text is readable. If the deployed UI shows obvious mojibake/garbled Chinese in major visible areas, fail phase4.

codex_connectivity_completeness_check.purpose: Validate the existing Codex identity end-to-end after the server upgrade.
required_checks: Identity is still valid and readable as a community identity.
Group membership is still valid on the server side.
A real community-originated inbound event reaches the Codex webhook path.
Codex emits a proper agent reply that the community accepts.
A real round-trip closes on the deployed server.
do_not: Do not redesign or modify Codex onboarding in this task.
Do not treat local mock-only evidence as sufficient.
Do not modify Codex onboarding implementation.
Do not ignore visible frontend readability defects just because API/workflow checks pass.
working_principles: Phase3 is already complete; resume from phase4.
Post-deploy validation must be real and must include human-visible frontend quality gates.
If phase4 fails, stop there; do not continue to Codex validation.
Codex connectivity validation is about completeness of the existing connected identity, not new feature work.

deliverables.description: Evidence bundle for post-deploy validation and Codex connectivity completeness.
Detailed controller log for the resumed top-level validation task.

acceptance: Post-deploy validation passes all required release-scope checks, including frontend readability.
If post-deploy validation passes, Codex connectivity completeness is validated end-to-end with real evidence.
If any blocker appears, the first real blocker is precisely classified and the task stops there.
